data.title: These are the countries that could be most affected by new tariffs on autos
data.description:
New tariffs on pharmaceuticals, semis, and autos could affect more disproportionately imports from nations with a higher imbalance in trade, including Mexico, Japan, South Korea, and Europe.

President Donald Trump on Tuesday announced new "25% or higher" tariffs on imports of pharmaceuticals, semiconductors, and autos, and that he would reveal more about it on April 2.

The U.S. motor vehicles trade deficit is more imbalanced with Mexico, at almost $100B in imports vs. $5B in exports in value, according to the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis.

U.S. motor vehicles trade deficit with Japan is also imbalanced at $40B in imports vs. very low exports.

Canada, on the other hand, is the most balanced trade, slightly skewed towards more imports than exports in auto vehicles.

China is the only major trade partner where exports are slightly higher than imports.

Auto ETFs: (NASDAQ:DRIV), (NYSEARCA:KARS), (NASDAQ:CARZ), (NYSEARCA:EVAV), (CARU), (CARD)

Country ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWW), (NYSEARCA:EWJ), (NYSEARCA:EWY), (NYSEARCA:VGK), (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NYSEARCA:EWC), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NASDAQ:MCHI)

Big motor vehicle companies: (F), (TM), (HMC), (TSLA), (GM), (RIVN), (STLA), (LCID)

data.title: OpenAI crosses 400M users for ChatGPT amid DeepSeek challenge
data.description:
Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT)-backed OpenAI had 400 million weekly active users for ChatGPT as of February.

It is a 33% increase from the 300 million weekly active users as of Dec. 4, 2024, an OpenAI spokesperson said in an emailed statement to Seeking Alpha.

The company had over 2 million paying business users, compared to 1 million as of Sept. 5, the spokesperson added.

OpenAI noted that developer traffic was up two times in the last six months, and five times for its reasoning models since the launch of the o3 model.

“There’s an overall effect of people really wanting these tools, and seeing that these tools are really valuable," said OpenAI COO Brad Lightcap in an interview with CNBC.

OpenAI counts Uber, Morgan Stanley, Moderna and T-Mobile among some of its largest enterprise customers, the report added.

While the consumer business may grow faster as people can adopt it at will, the enterprise is in the “process of building up,” Lightcap noted, according to the report.

“There’s a buying cycle there, and a learning process that goes into scaling an enterprise business,” said Lightcap. “AI is going to be like cloud services. It’s going to be something that you can’t run a business that ultimately is not really running on these very powerful models underneath the surface,” added Lightcap, as per the report.

Apple's (AAPL) integration of OpenAI's AI model started in December last year. Apple Intelligence relies on a mix of Apple’s own AI technology and a collaboration with OpenAI.

OpenAI’s growth comes amid competition from Chinese AI startup DeepSeek (DEEPSEEK). Tech stocks plunged on Jan. 27, after DeepSeek last month unveiled its AI model R1 and detailed in a paper how large language models, or LLMs, could be built on tight budgets and improved without human supervision. Nvidia (NVDA) saw around $589B in market cap erased on Jan. 27.

“DeepSeek is a testament to how much AI is like entered the public consciousness in the mainstream -- it would have been unfathomable two years ago,” said Lightcap. “It’s a moment that shows how powerful these models are and how much people really care.”

Earlier this week, several executives noted that DeepSeek does not pose any meaningful threat to the businesses of AI companies such as OpenAI and Anthropic, which is backed by Alphabet's (GOOG) (GOOGL) and Amazon (AMZN).

Last week, Google's AI head, reportedly, told employees that he was not worried about China’s DeepSeek AI and noted that the U.S. company has superior AI technology,

Lightcap also commented on the unsolicited $97.4B offer by a group led by Elon Musk, which was rejected by OpenAI.

“The numbers tell the story,” Lightcap noted. “We try to be very transparent about where we stand on all of this. [Musk] is a competitor. He’s competing. It’s an unorthodox way of competing, the report added.”

data.title: Digital Brands submits application to uplist to a national securities exchange
data.description:
Digital Brands (OTC:DBGI) has submitted an application to list its common stock on a national securities exchange, it said on Thursday.

"We believe that an uplisting to a national securities exchange is the next logical step in our growth journey," stated Hil Davis, CEO of Digital Brands Group.

No assurance can be given that Digital Brands Group’s listing application will be approved or that such listing will be completed, the company added.

“We believe that Digital Brands Group’s common stock will continue to be quoted on the OTC Pink under its current symbol DBGI pending any uplisting.”

data.title: Xerox slashes dividend by 50% to $0.125
data.description:
• Xerox (NASDAQ:XRX) declares $0.125/share quarterly dividend, -50% decrease from prior dividend of $0.250.
  • Forward yield 6.28%
  • Payable April 30; for shareholders of record March 31; ex-div March 31.
  • The board also declared a quarterly cash dividend of $20 per share on the outstanding Series A Convertible Perpetual Preferred Stock, payable on April 1, 2025 to shareholders of record on March 15, 2025.
  • See XRX Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Radcom outlines 2025 revenue growth of 12%-15% with strong 5G expansion focus
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Radcom Ltd. (NASDAQ:RDCM) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Benny Eppstein highlighted that 2024 marked the company's fifth consecutive year of growth, achieving record-breaking revenue and profitability. He noted that earnings per share grew over 75% on a GAAP basis, and cash and short-term deposits reached $94.7 million, the highest in the company's history. The company remains debt-free.
  • Radcom secured a multi-year contract with Norlys, replacing an incumbent assurance vendor. This deal expands Radcom's footprint in Europe and demonstrates its AI-enhanced assurance solutions' competitive advantage for 4G and 5G networks.
  • Eppstein emphasized the company's strategy to target mid-tier operators by productizing its 5G assurance solution, RADCOM ACE, into scalable packages to meet diverse operator needs. He also pointed to ongoing collaborations with ServiceNow to enhance telecom operators' service efficiency through AI-driven automation.
  • CFO Hadar Rahav stated, "We are pleased with how our team closed the year as we grow profitably," adding that the fourth quarter concluded with record revenue of $16.3 million, a 16.1% year-over-year increase. Gross margin was 75%, reflecting a stable revenue mix.

Outlook

  • For 2025, Radcom projects full-year revenue growth of 12%-15%, with a midpoint of $69.2 million, representing a 13.5% increase from 2024. Management attributed this growth to a strong backlog, multi-year contracts, and expanding opportunities in 5G assurance.
  • The company plans to increase its R&D investment to advance automation and Generative AI capabilities while maintaining operating profitability. Management expressed confidence in sustaining growth momentum through strategic partnerships and mid-tier market penetration.

Financial Results

  • Fourth-quarter revenue was $16.3 million, reflecting a 16.1% year-over-year increase. Operating income reached $2.9 million, representing 18% of revenue. Net income for Q4 remained steady at $3.8 million. On a GAAP basis, net income was $2.2 million, a decrease compared to the prior year due to higher tax expenses.
  • For the full year 2024, Radcom achieved record revenue of $61 million, up 82% from 2023. Net income totaled $13.5 million, or $0.83 per diluted share, compared to $10.2 million in 2023. GAAP net income was $7 million, or $0.43 per diluted share, a significant improvement from $3.7 million in 2023.
  • The company ended 2024 with $94.7 million in cash and equivalents, driven by $12.5 million in positive cash flow during the year.

Q&A

  • Linda Lee, William Blair: "Can you give us more color on the Norlys deal and its significance for geographic expansion?" CEO Eppstein explained that the deal introduces Radcom to mid-tier markets in Europe and demonstrates efficiencies using GenAI capabilities. He expressed optimism about additional European opportunities.
  • Lee followed up: "How do you feel about current sales capacity? Any plans to expand?" Eppstein confirmed plans to expand sales capacity and partnerships, particularly through ServiceNow collaborations and mid-tier product offerings.
  • Jeff Hopson, Needham & Company: "How are you balancing operating leverage and investments in R&D?" Eppstein stated that the company aims to maintain profitability while increasing R&D investment to develop advanced GenAI capabilities.
  • Hopson also asked about potential acquisitions. Eppstein noted that the company is evaluating opportunities carefully to expand its addressable market but has no immediate plans for M&A activity.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts displayed a positive tone regarding Radcom's progress, focusing on revenue growth, strategic deals, and market expansion. Their questions reflected confidence in the company's direction while seeking clarity on growth strategies.
  • Management's tone was confident during prepared remarks, emphasizing milestones and future opportunities. However, in Q&A, responses were cautious and measured, particularly regarding acquisitions and sales capacity expansion. Statements like "we are not in a hurry" and "we will announce more in the coming weeks" indicated a deliberate approach.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone reflected greater confidence in operational execution and strategic initiatives, backed by record-breaking results and strong financial positioning.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue grew from $15.8 million in Q3 2024 to $16.3 million in Q4 2024, driven by strong contract execution and market demand.
  • The Norlys deal in Q4 marked a significant step in geographic expansion, compared to Q3's focus on a North American operator contract fueled by Continual's technology.
  • Management's tone in Q4 was more confident, reflecting improved financial results and strategic clarity. Analysts' focus shifted to mid-tier market opportunities and partnerships, contrasting with Q3's emphasis on 5G technology trends.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged potential risks related to the dollar-shekel exchange rate but noted that stabilization at current levels eliminates the need for hedging.
  • Analysts sought clarity on the scalability of new contracts and the timeline for revenue recognition, highlighting the long sales cycles in the telecom sector.
  • Future grants from the Israel Innovation Authority remain uncertain, as current programs near completion.

Final Takeaway

Radcom closed 2024 with record-breaking results, driven by strategic contracts like Norlys and enhanced demand for its AI-driven 5G assurance solutions. The company projects 12%-15% revenue growth for 2025, supported by ongoing product innovation, geographic expansion, and strategic collaborations with partners like ServiceNow. Management's focus on scalable solutions for mid-tier operators positions Radcom for sustained growth in the evolving telecom landscape.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: BitFuFu buys majority stake in data center in Oklahoma, expanding U.S. mining footprint
data.description:
BitFuFu (NASDAQ:FUFU) has acquired a majority ownership of a 51-megawatt (MW) operational Bitcoin mining data center in Oklahoma, it said on Thursday.

The transaction, priced at $400,000 per MW, is funded with cash from BitFuFu’s balance sheet and common stock.

The facility boasts a track record of over 95% uptime and operates with a highly competitive net electricity cost of approximately $0.03/kWh.

This will enable BitFuFu to mine one Bitcoin at a cash cost as low as $18,000 at this site, assuming the deployment of AntMiner S21 XP, excluding depreciation of miners and facilities, the Bitcoin miner said.

BitFuFu plans to deploy AntMiner S21 series or higher efficiency version of miners at this site, which will increase its total managed hashrate scale by over 3EH/s.

data.title: Amplitude jumps on upbeat 2025 earnings forecast; brokerages raise rating
data.description:
Shares of Amplitude (NASDAQ:AMPL) jumped on Thursday as the digital analytics platform provider forecasted an upbeat 2025 and posted better-than-expected results a day earlier.

Following the blowout results, D.A. Davidson raised its rating on Amplitude to Buy and hiked its price target to $16.00 from $12.50.

"Amplitude exited '24 with strong enterprise momentum and positive indicators that current top-line reacceleration is durable in 2025 and beyond, the investment banking firm said.

Management's initial 2025 guidance of $324.8M-$330.8M implied a 10% Y/Y growth at the midpoint compared to D.A. Davidson's prior projection of 8.5% and consensus of 7.6%, Analyst Clark Wright commented.

Financial services company Baird, too, grew favorable of Amplitude, raising its rating on the company to Outperform and moving its price target higher to $17.00.

"While the macro still represents challenges, AMPL is in a very strong position to capture any increase in spend," said the financial services company.

"Operationally, AMPL is emerging as a multiproduct company. The recent acquisition of Command AI and launch of Guides and Surveys reinforces our view," Baird added.

Since the start of the year, Amplitude has gained about 30.5%. Seeking Alpha analysts and Wall Street are bullish on the company, while SA Quant has recommended the company as a Hold.

data.title: Affirm, Shopify announce expanded global agreement
data.description:
• Payments company Affirm Holdings (NASDAQ:AFRM) and internet infrastructure services provider Shopify (NYSE:SHOP) (TSX:SHOP:CA) announced on Thursday an expanded global agreement.
  • The renewed partnership enables Affirm to continue as the exclusive pay-over-time provider for Shop Pay Installments in the U.S. and Canada.
  • The partnership also plans to grow into new markets, with the plan to enter the U.K. on the horizon.

data.title: FTAI Aviation soars after review says short seller claims meritless
data.description:
FTAI Aviation (NASDAQ:FTAI) jumped 22% after the aerospace firm said that the allegations made against it by a short seller are without merit.

The Board's Audit Committee has completed its review, conducted by independent legal and forensic accounting advisors, and determined the short seller claims were meritless, the company said in a statement on Thursday. The company expects to file its Form 10-K timely.

"After a thorough and comprehensive review with the support of our independent legal and forensic accounting advisors, we have determined that the assertions made in the short seller reports are unsupported and have no merit," Paul R. Goodwin, Chair of the Audit Committee, said in a statement.

FTAI Aviation (NASDAQ:FTAI) dropped 25% on Jan. 21 after the company's board determined that it will start a review in response to allegations from a Muddy Waters' short report from last month. That report sent FTAI's shares plunging 25% on Jan. 15.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Southern Company beats Q4 top-line estimates; initiates FY25 outlook
